title: FIFA Banned Merlín the Duck From Mexico's Match
script: FIFA banned... a duck? Merlín — a 2-year-old duck in a tiny green Mexico jersey — became the unofficial mascot of the 2026 World Cup. He showed up at Mexico's opening match and went instantly viral. Got invited to the presidential palace. Netflix. Televisa. He waddled through Mexico City streets while fans chanted his name. Then his owner brought him to Azteca Stadium for the Czech Republic match. Cameras set up outside. A FIFA spokesperson confirmed it: live animals aren't allowed inside the stadium. Merlín could film a commercial on the grounds. But not watch the game. In 2 weeks, that duck visited more Mexico City landmarks than most tourists manage in a month. Should the World Cup have a duck exemption?
